name: Hydra (Composable Config for ML Projects)

performance.notes: Config composition at startup; no runtime overhead

agent_readiness.notes: @hydra.main decorator. YAML configs with OmegaConf. Config groups for composable configs (model=resnet, dataset=cifar10). Command-line override: python train.py model=vit lr=0.001. Multirun sweep: python train.py -m lr=0.001,0.01,0.1. Structured configs (dataclass-based schema). Plugins: Optuna for hyperparameter sweep. Widely used in ML research (PyTorch, Meta). Steeper learning curve but powerful for experiment management. Not needed for simple apps.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — OmegaConf interpolation syntax
Config group composition can be confusing for newcomers
Adds startup overhead for config composition
Not suitable for non-ML web apps
best_when: ML research with many experiment configurations
Hyperparameter sweeps across multiple config combinations
PyTorch Lightning or Hugging Face Trainer-based projects
Teams running grid searches across model/data/training configs
avoid_when: Web applications or microservices (dynaconf or pydantic-settings better)
Simple scripts with few config parameters
Teams unfamiliar with YAML composition concepts
summary: Hydra is Meta's composable configuration framework for ML experiments. @hydra.main with YAML configs, config groups (model=resnet dataset=cifar10), CLI overrides, and multirun sweeps (lr=0.001,0.01,0.1). OmegaConf backend. Structured dataclass configs. Optuna plugin for HPO. Standard in ML research. Steep learning curve but powerful for experiment management. Web apps: use dynaconf or pydantic-settings.
